description: Need to verify a restore worked — got a backup at /home/user/backups/customers.json and the live copy at /home/user/data/customers.csv. They should have the same customer IDs. Just dump the IDs from both into /home/user/verify/json_ids.txt and /home/user/verify/csv_ids.txt, one per line, sorted. I'll diff them myself.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/backups/customers.json exists, contains a JSON array of objects each with an "id" field (integers), plus other fields (name, email, etc.). 50 records total. Example: [{"id": 1042, "name": "Acme Corp", ...}, ...]
- /home/user/data/customers.csv exists, has a header row with columns: id,name,email,created_at. Same 50 customer IDs as the JSON (restore was successful). id is the first column.
- /home/user/verify/ directory exists but is empty.
- jq, python3, awk, sed, sort, cut all available.
- /home/user writable.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/verify/json_ids.txt exists, contains exactly 50 lines, each line a single integer ID extracted from the JSON, sorted numerically ascending, no duplicates, no blank lines.
- /home/user/verify/csv_ids.txt exists, contains exactly 50 lines, each line a single integer ID extracted from the CSV (excluding the header), sorted numerically ascending, no duplicates, no blank lines.
- Both files contain the same IDs (since the backup matches).

Invariants:
- /home/user/backups/customers.json unchanged.
- /home/user/data/customers.csv unchanged.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- json_ids.txt must have exactly 50 lines: `wc -l < /home/user/verify/json_ids.txt` returns 50.
- csv_ids.txt must have exactly 50 lines: `wc -l < /home/user/verify/csv_ids.txt` returns 50.
- Files must be sorted numerically: `sort -n /home/user/verify/json_ids.txt | diff - /home/user/verify/json_ids.txt` exits 0, same for csv_ids.txt.
- IDs must be integers only (no quotes, no trailing fields): `grep -E '^[0-9]+$' /home/user/verify/json_ids.txt | wc -l` returns 50, same for csv_ids.txt.
